steps.title: Use the Round Table timeline
steps.content: The passage says Wace added the Round Table when adapting Geoffrey of Monmouth's History into Roman de Brut in 1155. If Wace introduced that feature then, the passage supports an inference that earlier Arthurian sources did not already include it.

steps.title: Reject unsupported extensions
steps.content: Choice A makes an unsupported claim about historical accuracy and relies on Culhwch and Olwen in a way the passage does not support. Choice B says historians know Round Table stories were available to Geoffrey, but the passage suggests the opposite by saying Wace introduced the Round Table later. Choice C compares the overall content of several works, which the passage never does. Choice D connects the 11th-century Vita Sancti Cadoci to the timeline: it predates Wace's 1155 addition, so its source legends would not have featured the Round Table. The answer is D.
